(07:21 GMT) *Correct: Hermes 2024 Net Pft EUR4.60B
(07:21 GMT) Birkin Bag Maker Hermes Posts Higher Revenue on Strong Americas Performance

By Andrea Figueras

French luxury group Hermes reported higher sales for the final quarter of 2024 after a strong performance in the Americas, adding to signs of a potential rebound in demand for luxury goods.

The maker of the Birkin bag reported on Friday revenue of 3.96 billion euros ($4.15 billion) for the fourth quarter, up nearly 18% at constant exchange rates compared with the prior-year period.

The result compares with analysts' expectations of 3.73 billion euros, according to a Visible Alpha poll of estimates.

For the year as a whole, the group made earnings before interest and taxes of 6.15 billion euros compared with 5.65 billion euros previously. Net profit jumped to 4.60 billion euros, surpassing analysts' prospects of 4.51 billion euros.

The company confirmed its expectations for the medium-term of revenue growth at constant exchange rates, but cautioned on global economic, geopolitical and monetary uncertainties.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Porsche to Cut 1,900 Jobs in Germany by 2029
data.description:
Porsche plans to axe 1,900 jobs at two German sites by 2029 amid a decrease in demand for electric vehicles, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing the company.

The German luxury automaker intends to reduce 15% of its workforce at its plants in Zuffenhausen and Weissach through voluntary measures, including early retirement and severance packages, and implement a "restrictive approach" to new hiring.

data.title: German Wholesale Prices Rise the Most in 1-½-Years
data.description:
Germany’s wholesale prices rose by 0.9% year-on-year in January 2025, accelerating from a 0.1% gain in the previous month.

This marks the second consecutive period of growth and was the fastest since April 2023, mainly due to higher wholesale costs of non-ferrous ores, metals and semi-finished metal products (+24.4%).

Coffee, tea, cocoa, and spices also saw a significant price increase of 34.4%, while sugar, confectionery, and bakery products rose by 11%.

Milk, dairy products, eggs, edible fats, and oils were also up 7%.

In contrast, wholesale prices fell for iron, steel, and ferrous semi-finished metal products (-6.3%), as well as computers and peripheral equipment (-6.2%), and chemical products (-2.6%).

On a monthly basis, wholesales prices grew 0.9%, the most since September 2022, picking up from a 0.1% rise in December.

data.title: CVC Capital Partners Reports a Rise in Fee-Paying Assets Under Management
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

CVC Capital Partners said fee-paying assets under management increased in 2024 as investment activity recovered, but added that it remains cautious on its near-term outlook.

The private equity company said Friday that as of Dec. 31, fee-paying assets under management--which represents the total value of assets under management on which management fees are charged--had increased to 147 billion euros ($153.87 billion). This compares with 98 billion euros as at Dec. 31, 2023.

In a full-year activity update, CVC said assets under management reached 200 billion euros by the end of last year.

The company said it remained cautious on the near-term outlook for exits from investments.

Over 2024, CVC said investment activity recovered strongly and that it was pleased with the overall resilience of its investment portfolios.

It is scheduled to publish its full-year results on March. 20.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Earnings Flash (CCEP.L) Coca-Cola European Partners Reports FY24 EPS EUR3.08
Shein's IPO Plans Could Be Delayed as Trump Mulls Crackdown on Small-Goods Imports, the Financial Times Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Online fast-fashion giant Shein could delay a potential initial public offering in London after President Trump announced a crackdown on tariff-free imports of small goods from China, The Financial Times reports, citing unnamed sources
  • The potential listing, which Shein had planned to take place as soon as this Easter, could be pushed into the second half of the year, according to the FT.
  • The uncertainty over impact and timing of regulatory changes, which has temporarily paused small-good imports, is weighing on Shein's IPO timetable, the FT reports.

Full story: https://bit.ly/4b7ilnZ

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Hang Seng Index Extends Gains; Up 3.3%
Hang Seng Tech Index Extends Gains; Up 5.0%
Allcargo Logistics Logs Lower Fiscal Q4 Consolidated Net Profit
data.description:
Allcargo Logistics consolidated profit attributable to the owners fell to 96.9 million Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter from 109.5 million rupees a year ago, according to an Indian bourse filing Thursday.

Earnings per share during the quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, slipped to 0.10 rupees from 0.11 rupees a year earlier.

Income from operations in fiscal Q3 increased to 41.1 billion rupees from 32.1 billion rupees a year ago.

data.title: Eurostoxx futures -0.3% in early European trading
data.description:
Key points:
  • German DAX futures -0.5%
  • French CAC 40 futures -0.3%
  • UK FTSE futures flat

Well, the drop ahead of the open only offers a bit of a scratch to the gains from yesterday. European indices are still sitting comfortably higher on the week itself and poised for a seventh straight week of gains. US futures are a touch higher though, with S&P 500 futures up 0.1% currently. The attention will turn towards the US retail sales data later as well as any more Trump headlines before the weekend.

data.title: CFI Cements Central Asia Presence: Opens New Baku Office
data.description:
CFI Financial Group, a contracts for differences (CFDs) broker with a strong presence in the Middle East, is now expanding into Central Asia by opening an office in Baku, the capital of Azerbaijan.

CFI Enters Azerbaijan

The new office was established after setting up a local entity, CFI Financial Investment Company (CFI Azerbaijan).

Its interest in the Azerbaijani market emerged last year when it acquired a local brokerage firm, AzFinance Investment Company. The brokerage giant then stated that it would rebrand the local firm to CFI, aligning it with its global brand.Hisham Mansour, Co-Founder and Managing Director. Source: CFI

“Azerbaijan represents a fast-growing financial market with immense potential,” said Hisham Mansour, Co-Founder and Managing Director of CFI Financial Group.

According to the World Bank, Azerbaijan has a population of over 10.1 million, with a per capita income of $22,640. The broker highlighted that launching CFI Azerbaijan will also help it expand in the surrounding region.

Earlier, CFI appointed Ilgar Rustambayl as the CEO of its Azerbaijan unit. Interestingly, he is an outsider to the trading industry and previously held management roles at the international offices of Philip Morris International, The Coca-Cola Company, and MIG Bank.

“The launch of our Baku office is more than just an expansion—it is a commitment to providing Azerbaijani traders and investors with a world-class trading environment featuring competitive conditions, advanced financial tools, and expert guidance,” Rustambayl said.

Expansion amid Demand Surge

Founded in 1998, CFI operates regulated entities in multiple jurisdictions, including the UK, UAE, Jordan, and Lebanon. The company offers retail trading services through its online platform, with features such as zero-spread accounts and commission-free trading on certain products.

Last December, CFI also launched its operations in South Africa and appointed Zihaad Israfil as the local CEO.

Meanwhile, trading volume on the brokerage platform surged last year. As Finance Magnates reported earlier, CFI handled $1.12 trillion in trading volume in the last three months of 2024, a record for the broker.

data.title: Tokyo Most Actives
China Longevity Gets Cash Partial Offer
data.description:
China Longevity Group received a letter stating Alternative Liquidity Index intends to make a pre-conditional voluntary cash partial offer to acquire up to 85,261,250, or 10% of the company's issued shares, at HK$0.01 apiece, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The offeror has requested consent from Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission for the offer, with the regulator's executive director of the corporate finance division indicating inclination to approve it.

The firm will hire an advisor to weigh in whether the offer is fair and reasonable.

Trading in the shares of the polyester fabric manufacturer has been suspended since Feb. 14, 2013.

data.title: Market Chatter: Xiaomi May Raise Price of Flagship Phone Due to Higher Costs
data.description:
Xiaomi could raise the price for its new flagship phone due to rising research and development and component costs, The Standard reported Thursday, citing founder Lei Jun.

Xiaomi 15 Ultra, to be launched this month, may exceed the previous generation's 6,499 yuan, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Hefei Department Store's Unit to Sell Real Estate Assets
data.description:
Hefei Department Store Group's unit, Anhui Taikelong Chain Supermarket, plans to publicly list and sell some of its idle real estate through the Anhui Changjiang Property Rights Exchange, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

The assets in Xuancheng Economic and Technological Development Zone, Xuancheng City, Anhui Province, China will include factory buildings, office buildings, and industrial land. The industrial park covers an area of ​​32,369.1 square meters.

The property has a market value of 28.4 million yuan based on April 3, 2024, the Chinese department store operator said.

The company's shares closed 2% lower.

data.title: Earnings Flash (NWG.L) NATWEST GROUP PLC Reports FY24 EPS GBX53.10
Wood Group Engaging With Lenders on Options
Wood Group Assessing All Potential Refinancing Options
Australian Shares Pare Early Gains
data.description:
The S&P/ASX 200 Index close 0.2% higher at 2,556 on Friday, trimming earlier gains.

The index had earlier hit an all-time high as investors cheered following US President Donald Trump’s decision to delay implementing reciprocal tariffs.

Strong corporate earnings from major US companies also supported the bullish sentiment, outweighing concerns over stronger-than-expected inflation figures in the US. Additionally, Australia’s resource-heavy bourse benefitted from rising commodity prices, fueled by a sharp decline in the US dollar and Treasury yields.

Among large-cap stocks, notable gains were seen from CSL Ltd (+1.1%), Wesfarmers (+0.8%), Aristocrat Leisure (+0.7%), and Zip Co (+0.9%).

The benchmark index rose 0.5% for the week and is up 0.3% so far this year.

AUDUSD increased to an 8-week high of 0.63.

Over the past 4 weeks, Australian Dollar US Dollar gained 1.63%, and in the last 12 months, it decreased 2.99%.

data.title: China's Healthcare Sector Could Ride the AI Wave — Market Talk
data.description: China's recent AI breakthrough could boost the efficiency of its healthcare sector and enable other potential novel services, Jefferies analyst Cui Cui says in an email. Chinese health-tech stocks are broadly higher in Hong Kong, with Alibaba Health Information up 25% and Ping An Health and Technology gaining 14%. Chinese contract drug makers WuXi AppTec and WuXi Biologics are 8.4% and 11% higher, respectively. Although these stocks aren't direct beneficiaries of the AI concept, they are likely rallying on their significant underweight positions and the fundamental shift in market sentiment after the Biosecure Act wasn't passed under the Biden administration, Cui adds. Her top industry picks in 2025 are WuXi Bio due to its better new order growth momentum and JD Health because of its AI development potential. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: ZINZINO AB (PUBL.): ANNOUNCES ACQUISITION OF ZURVITA'S ASSETS FOLLOWING SUCCESSFUL CHAPTER 11 PROCESS
data.description:
ZINZINO AB (PUBL.): ANNOUNCES ACQUISITION OF ZURVITA'S ASSETS FOLLOWING SUCCESSFUL CHAPTER 11 PROCESS

PR Newswire

GOTHENBURG, Sweden, Feb. 14, 2025

GOTHENBURG, Sweden, Feb. 14, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — In a press release dated December 21, 2024, Zinzino announced that the company has finalized an agreement to acquire the assets of Zurvita, a direct-selling healthcare company, after serving as a debtor-in-possession (DIP) financier during Zurvita's Chapter 11 bankruptcy proceedings. Zinzino provided DIP financing totaling USD 4.5 million, and following the successful completion of the restructuring process, its offer to acquire Zurvita's assets has been accepted as part of a debt-settled purchase arrangement. The final terms of the acquisition have now been agreed upon by both parties.

Under the agreement, Zinzino will pay a fixed purchase price of USD 9.4 million, which includes the DIP financing. Of this amount, USD 2.5 million will be settled through newly issued Zinzino shares. Additionally, the acquisition includes contingent earn-out payments based on sales performance from 2025 to 2029, with a maximum additional consideration of USD 1.9 million, which will also be fully settled in newly issued Zinzino shares. The cash portion of the purchase price will be financed through Zinzino's existing cash reserves.

Zurvita operates in the United States, Canada, and Mexico, offering a diverse portfolio of innovative health and wellness products. Through this acquisition, Zinzino will gain access to Zurvita's distribution network, inventory, intellectual property rights, and other key assets. While Zurvita's annual sales currently total approximately USD 30 million, the integration with Zinzino is expected to drive further growth through synergistic benefits, leveraging Zinzino's test-based product approach and extensive distributor network. Profitability is anticipated to improve through the optimization of Zinzino's existing technological platform and operational efficiencies.

This acquisition aligns with Zinzino's long-term strategy of sustainable, profitable growth, expansion into new markets, and continued enhancement of its product portfolio. Building on previous acquisitions, including VMA Life (2020), Enhanzz (2022), and the recent asset purchase of Xelliss, as well as its strategic partnership with ACN, Zinzino remains committed to strengthening its distribution capabilities and reinforcing its leadership in personalized, test-based nutrition.

"Personalized advice and tailored solutions are the future, not just in health and wellness," said Dag Bergheim Pettersen, CEO of Zinzino, and Jay Shafer, CEO and co-founder of Zurvita. "Together, we bring decades of industry expertise and a shared vision to revolutionize the modern, personalized shopping experience through direct sales."

For more information:

Dag Bergheim Pettersen CEO Zinzino, +47 (0) 932 25 700, www.zinzino.com

Pictures for publication free of charge:

marketing@zinzino.com

Certified Adviser:

Carnegie Investment Bank AB (publ.)

data.title: NatWest Sees 2025 RoTE 15%-16%
Saudi's Derayah Financial Prices Tadawul IPO
data.description:
Digital investment platform operator Derayah Financial said Friday it priced its initial public offering in Saudi Arabia at 30 riyals per share.

The offer price reflects a total volume of 1.5 billion Saudi riyals and implies a market capitalization of 7.5 billion riyals. The institutional bookbuilding garnered total orders of 243 billion riyals from local and international investors.

The subscription period for individual investors runs from Feb. 20 to Feb. 22.
